prediction_ref.short_label: 1-bit native training becomes the dominant alternative to post-hoc quantization (led by Bonsai, Qwen/Llama 1-bit derivatives to follow)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Today's window only re-airs the existing Bonsai-8B GGUF and the Apr 18 PrismML CUDA tutorial; no new 1-bit native training launches and no Qwen / Llama 1-bit derivatives surface.

reference_links.label: MarkTechPost - Coding Tutorial for PrismML Bonsai 1-Bit LLM on CUDA

bridge.narrative: Bonsai-8B's continued visibility (1.15 GB, 12-14x smaller, 6x faster on RTX 4090) keeps the precondition observation alive but the window adds no new 1-bit native training launch and no Qwen or Llama 1-bit derivative, so the underlying claim continues without fresh acceleration.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a second lab to ship a 1-bit-from-scratch checkpoint or a Qwen / Llama 1-bit fork to confirm the trend.

prediction_ref.short_label: Convergence on an "Agent Registry" model (registry standard for distributing skills, tool permissions, audit traces)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: OpenClaw 2026.4.20-beta tightens scoping and untrusted-workspace key blocking, NemoClaw guidance is reaffirmed, and Hermes Agent Tool Gateway adds OSV-based MCP malware scans, but no cross-vendor registry spec or shared artifact format appears.

reference_links.label: DEV Community - Hermes Agent Review: 95.6K Stars

bridge.narrative: Per-vendor agent runtimes keep maturing in parallel: OpenClaw tightens scoping, NemoClaw stays as the enterprise wrapper, and Hermes ships a Tool Gateway with MCP OAuth 2.1 and OSV malware scans. The structural-force claim still holds, but no shared registry artifact format crosses vendors yet.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a multi-vendor spec draft or a portable skill / tool-permission artifact format crossing OpenClaw, NemoClaw, and Hermes.

prediction_ref.short_label: Indirect prompt injection becomes a top CVE category (CVSS 8.0+ LLM-integration CVEs become Patch Tuesday regulars)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Three reinforcing items land in 24 hours: OpenClaw CVE-2026-41329 (CVSS 9.9 sandbox bypass), Google Antigravity prompt-injection-to-RCE via fd's -X flag, and continued SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) reporting — all show LLM-integration codepaths reaching Patch-Tuesday-grade severity.

reference_links.label: CCB Belgium - Warning: Privilege Escalation in OpenClaw
CSO Online - Prompt injection turned Google's Antigravity file search into RCE

bridge.narrative: Three concurrent CVSS 9.8+ LLM-integration CVEs land inside the window — OpenClaw sandbox bypass, Antigravity prompt-injection RCE, and continuing SGLang SSTI — exactly the Patch-Tuesday-grade cadence the prediction projects, with CCB Belgium even issuing an emergency advisory.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a confirmed Patch Tuesday rollup that lists multiple LLM-integration CVEs in a single monthly bundle, not just clustered weekly disclosures.

prediction_ref.short_label: Acceleration of "local > cloud" inversion (by Q3 2026, individual / SMB coding agents standardize on local-default)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Hugging Face ml-intern lifts Qwen3-1.7B's GPQA from 8.5% to 32% in under 10 hours and beats Claude Code's 22.99%; the Qwen3.6 GitHub repo and Bonsai-8B 1-bit GGUF on llama.cpp b8862 keep pushing the local-runnable coding-agent envelope.

bridge.narrative: ml-intern lifting Qwen3-1.7B GPQA from 8.5% to 32% in under 10 hours and overtaking Claude Code's 22.99% is direct evidence that local post-training now produces credible coding-agent quality, reinforcing the precondition that edge models match useful agent quality bars.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a major IDE or coding-agent vendor to ship a local-default config flag, not just better local benchmarks.

prediction_ref.short_label: Secret leakage through CI / agents becomes the next battleground ("Agent-in-the-Loop Secret Exfiltration" rises to top of CIS / OWASP risk)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Vercel confirms a supply-chain breach traced to Context.ai's stolen Google Workspace OAuth tokens with the CEO citing AI-assisted speed; Antigravity's prompt-injection-to-RCE via fd shows the same agent-in-the-loop exfil shape from a different angle.

reference_links.label: Trend Micro - The Vercel Breach: OAuth Supply Chain
The Register - Vercel breach traced to stolen employee creds

bridge.narrative: Vercel's confirmed supply-chain breach via Context.ai OAuth tokens — with the CEO pointing to AI-assisted attacker speed — and Antigravity's prompt-injection-to-RCE both materialize the agent-in-the-loop exfil shape, putting concrete real-world breaches behind the precondition that ties CI / agents to leaked secrets.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need an explicit CIS or OWASP entry naming the category, not only vendor-side incident reports.

prediction_ref.short_label: "Headless Everything" architecture standardizes (headless-preferred metadata on MCP / Agent Registry)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: OpenAI's Codex update emphasizes computer use, terminals, SSH devboxes, and in-app browsing rather than MCP metadata; ChatGPT Images 2.0 ships native reasoning at 2K resolution. Neither moves the registry-side headless-preferred metadata story.

bridge.narrative: Today's headline agent moves are GUI-driving and visual: Codex expands to terminals, SSH devboxes, and in-app browsing, while ChatGPT Images 2.0 prioritizes 2K reasoning over service-call metadata. Neither pushes the registry-side headless-preferred metadata story, leaving the prediction neither supported nor refuted.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a concrete MCP or agent-registry server metadata field carrying headless-preferred semantics.

prediction_ref.short_label: GGUF supply chains as new battleground (HF/Ollama/ModelScope standardize chat_template signature verification)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 keeps reproducing in the window with `ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment` recommended as mitigation, and Bonsai-8B continues as the headline 1-bit GGUF on Hugging Face; no hub or runtime has yet announced default-on signature verification or sandbox-by-default loading.

bridge.narrative: SGLang CVE-2026-5760's continued reporting and the explicit ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment mitigation recommendation reinforce the structural fact that GGUF chat_templates carry executable code on load, but no hub or serving runtime has yet announced default-on signature verification or sandboxed loading.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need an explicit Hugging Face, Ollama Library, or vLLM / Bedrock policy switch turning on signature verification or SSTI scanning by default.

prediction_ref.short_label: The era of "exclusive alliance between hyperscaler and frontier lab" (5-pole regime locks in; Trainium / TPU / MAIA / MI300X design differences translate to model-behavior differences)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Apr 21-22 expansion of the Anthropic-AWS deal ($100B / 10yr, 5 GW Trainium, $25B Amazon investment, AMZN +2-3% to $253.62), plus reports that Anthropic is exploring its own AI chip and Google's TPU 8t / 8i split with Broadcom and MediaTek as design partners — all crystallize the 5-pole hyperscaler-lab-silicon shape.

reference_links.label: Anthropic - Anthropic and Amazon expand collaboration
tradingkey - Anthropic Moving Toward AI Chips for Claude

bridge.narrative: The Anthropic-AWS expansion to $100B / 10yr with 5 GW Trainium plus Amazon's $25B equity, Anthropic exploring its own custom silicon, and Google's TPU 8t / 8i split with Broadcom and MediaTek directly materialize the codesigned-exclusive precondition for the 5-pole regime, with AMZN's 2-3% pop pricing in the long-horizon AWS commit.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need explicit lock-in for Meta-internal and xAI-Oracle/NVDA poles at the same scale and a runtime-agnostic backend portability layer adopted at production scale.

prediction_ref.short_label: Proprietary re-expansion against an open-weight geopolitical split ("strongest model = closed weights" crystallizes)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Qwen3.6-Max-Preview hosted-only continues with deltas significant on agentic coding / world knowledge / instruction following; AISI's Mythos Preview evaluation lands inside the window with Project Glasswing-only access (AWS / Apple / Google / JPMC / Microsoft / NVIDIA), reinforcing the staged-gating closed-tier shape.

bridge.narrative: Qwen3.6-Max-Preview's hosted-only stance with statistically significant agentic-coding deltas plus AISI's Mythos Preview evaluation under Project Glasswing partner-only access (AWS / Apple / Google / JPMC / Microsoft / NVIDIA) directly extend the precondition that frontier labs gate top-tier models behind staged closed access while open-weight cadence stays at the 35B-A3B class.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a third frontier lab to publicly confirm an API-only flagship release before Q3 2026.
